metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a separate proving command for the rebar-ticket trailer sub-claim (e.g. `git log -1 --format='%B' | grep -E 'rebar-ticket: ade2-8c73-e010-491d'` exits 0) or note that AC7's Gerrit Verified gate covers it.
findings.finding: AC6 bundles two distinct sub-claims — 'commit references this ticket (rebar-ticket trailer)' and 'passes make lint' — but provides only one proving command (`make lint`). If `make lint` does not verify commit trailers (typical for lint tooling), the trailer sub-claim has no proving command and is unverified until Gerrit review, creating a silent gap.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC6: 'Commit references this ticket (rebar-ticket trailer) and passes `make lint`.'
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC6 into two lines: (a) `git log -1 --format='%B' | grep -qE 'rebar-ticket:[[:space:]]*ade2-8c73-e010-491d'` exits 0; (b) `make lint` exits 0. Or add a parenthetical: '(trailer checked by Gerrit Verified +1 in AC7)' to make the coverage explicit.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or add to the plan) that `maybe_drain` logs or meters swallowed exceptions at a visible level (e.g. `logger.warning` + a counter) so silent enrichment failures are observable without source-diving.
findings.finding: The plan states `maybe_drain` 'swallows all exceptions' but does not specify that swallowed exceptions are logged, metered, or traced — so silent enrichment failures (LLM down, quota exhausted, storage errors) produce no observable signal for operators or CI.
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's 'Mitigation / bounding' section, add: 'Swallowed exceptions in `maybe_drain` are logged at WARNING level with the exception type and incremented on a `rebar.enrich_drain.error_total` counter, ensuring silent failures are observable via logs/metrics without blocking writes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the plan (or the referenced epic) specifies a RETIRE/cleanup path for BM25F digest entries (e.g. TTL-based eviction, ticket-close hook, or explicit size cap) so the digest store does not accumulate unboundedly when this flag is enabled.
findings.finding: The plan activates opportunistic accumulation of BM25F digests in a shared store but does not describe a RETIRE/cleanup path for that accumulated state — digests could grow unboundedly or become stale without a defined eviction or pruning mechanism.
findings.location: Behavioral activation — path #2 ('Opportunistic enrichment drain') and Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate all three valid `overlap_drain` values (`off` / `async` / `always`) as a closed set in the Mitigation section, with a one-line description of each mode's behavior.
findings.finding: The plan names `overlap_drain = "off"` and `overlap_drain = "async"` (default) as the two control values for write-path enrichment, but never discloses the third valid enum value `"always"` (the synchronous/inline mode used in CI and tests). The code-verified closed set is `{"off", "async", "always"}` (`src/rebar/llm/config.py:458`). A contributor reading only the plan cannot discover the CI/inline mode, and the plan's claim that `overlap_drain = "off"` produces "overlap WITHOUT write-path enrichment" is the only migration path named — the `"always"` mode (which changes timing, not the flag) is entirely absent.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Mitigation section, replace the parenthetical with: "`overlap_drain` accepts exactly three values: `"async"` (default — detached subprocess), `"always"` (synchronous inline, for CI/tests), `"off"` (no write-path enrichment at all)."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to the Behavioral activation section (or AC5) clarifying that `overlap[]` is populated only on a full sidecar-emitting run (`emit_sidecar=True`), and that pure-read calls (`emit_sidecar=False`) skip the overlap step even when `overlap_enabled=true`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 and behavioral description treat `overlap_enabled = true` as the sole gate for the `overlap[]` key appearing in the verdict. However, the code imposes a second independent boolean gate: `overlap[]` is only added inside `if emit_sidecar:` (plan_review/__init__.py:510) — it is not populated on a pure-read call (`emit_sidecar=False`). The plan never names `emit_sidecar` as a precondition. AC5's verification command works because the CLI defaults `emit_sidecar=True`, but the plan's prose claim that flipping `overlap_enabled` activates the overlap step is structurally incomplete: the step is silently skipped whenever `emit_sidecar=False`, regardless of the flag.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Behavioral activation section after 'Plan-review overlap step', add: 'Note: the overlap step runs only when `emit_sidecar=True` (the default for `rebar review-plan`); pure-read library calls with `emit_sidecar=False` skip it regardless of the flag.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `overlap` as a named property to `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` (array of objects, `additionalProperties: true`) with a description that states it is advisory-only and never affects the verdict or claim gate, so the processing rule is schema-co-located.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` schema does not declare `overlap` as a named property — the key is an undocumented extension riding on `additionalProperties: true`. The plan's processing rule ('`overlap[]` is ADVISORY ONLY, never blocks a claim or close') is defined only in plan prose, not co-located with the schema property definition. This violates the T8 criterion that a processing protocol/decision rule be co-located with the schema that needs it.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` properties: `"overlap": { "type": "array", "description": "Advisory store-wide cross-ticket overlap suggestions (epic only-crave-art). Present only when overlap_enabled=true and emit_sidecar=true. NEVER blocks a claim or affects the verdict.", "items": { "type": "object", "additionalProperties": true } }`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
